metadata.user_prompt: in every frame attempt to identify if the user is looking for packages.

transcript.text: What's up y'all and welcome to my first YouTube video  So what I'm going to be showing you guys is how I usually do my deliveries and how I load up my van  But as you can see I might this is how my van is looking today and  Today we got one thirty eight stops are in the you see and two fifty two forty nine packages today which is pretty light  New usual  But yeah, so this is how my van is looking yeah, this is how my van is basically how it looks already set up my first bag  Which it's in my first off which the house is like right here and  Yeah, and see see what's gonna happen. I let's do the first delivery or I mean the first off  Which is one  You six also the way I organize my my bag and see there I put the boxes in the and the back of it  And my over my envelopes in the front which is like that's it's  One two five one ten that's basically how I do it and I use usually this is a you  I'm exceed so you you're 16 the driver eight number and  They're usually I separate those but yeah, this is my first delivery  Should be this house  No worries that's pretty easy until next one  I'm already done with the first bag so I'm open which is it  918 I'm one eight right here. I didn't really organize my my bags. I just throw a man  Yeah, I don't like organize them straw man  Whatever you know what I mean the way down to the next stuff. It's always so annoying  Yeah, first up  What do you mean the streets are really small like usually there's no parking and not  And there's since I just park in the middle of the street and then blocked the whole fucking street and  I hope you honest. I just leave it at the front door. I'm sorry not to a phone  So next one  Oh my God, scared me usually the tree. You see this. I was right here  Let's give the  Yeah, sir. I got me something. I got 45 left a stop-slip and got 80 more packages  Which is it's not so bad. I should finish around what seven or six 30 around there  But it's pretty much you know any any Amazon driver case this is this map GPS whatever  It's just jumping it's just jumping all over the place. I think it's not working properly  Amazon like it's your shit now  It's already 717. I got three more stops left 12 packages. Which is crazy  And that's it. I'm already done with my route  I got zero packages. I'm gonna swap on about about this batch. It is 730  I don't know what happened. I took too long  Yeah, you know hope you guys enjoyed the video. Make sure to subscribe and leave a like  Thank you. I'm gonna head back to the station though  You see what's up  Hopefully if not I had to go rescue them. Yeah, yeah

segments.text: What's up y'all and welcome to my first YouTube video
So what I'm going to be showing you guys is how I usually do my deliveries and how I load up my van
But as you can see I might this is how my van is looking today and
Today we got one thirty eight stops are in the you see and two fifty two forty nine packages today which is pretty light
New usual
But yeah, so this is how my van is looking yeah, this is how my van is basically how it looks already set up my first bag
Which it's in my first off which the house is like right here and
Yeah, and see see what's gonna happen. I let's do the first delivery or I mean the first off
Which is one
You six also the way I organize my my bag and see there I put the boxes in the and the back of it
And my over my envelopes in the front which is like that's it's
One two five one ten that's basically how I do it and I use usually this is a you
I'm exceed so you you're 16 the driver eight number and
They're usually I separate those but yeah, this is my first delivery
Should be this house
No worries that's pretty easy until next one
I'm already done with the first bag so I'm open which is it
918 I'm one eight right here. I didn't really organize my my bags. I just throw a man
Yeah, I don't like organize them straw man
Whatever you know what I mean the way down to the next stuff. It's always so annoying
Yeah, first up
What do you mean the streets are really small like usually there's no parking and not
And there's since I just park in the middle of the street and then blocked the whole fucking street and
I hope you honest. I just leave it at the front door. I'm sorry not to a phone
So next one
Oh my God, scared me usually the tree. You see this. I was right here
Let's give the
Yeah, sir. I got me something. I got 45 left a stop-slip and got 80 more packages
Which is it's not so bad. I should finish around what seven or six 30 around there
But it's pretty much you know any any Amazon driver case this is this map GPS whatever
It's just jumping it's just jumping all over the place. I think it's not working properly
Amazon like it's your shit now
It's already 717. I got three more stops left 12 packages. Which is crazy
And that's it. I'm already done with my route
I got zero packages. I'm gonna swap on about about this batch. It is 730
I don't know what happened. I took too long
Yeah, you know hope you guys enjoyed the video. Make sure to subscribe and leave a like
Thank you. I'm gonna head back to the station though
You see what's up
Hopefully if not I had to go rescue them. Yeah, yeah

frame_analyses.response:
Frame 0
Setting/Scene: The scene is indoors, looking down a narrow aisle or hallway lined with shelves stacked with cardboard boxes. The lighting suggests an interior space, possibly a storage area or back room.
Action/Movement: The camera is positioned low, looking down the aisle. The movement appears to be a slow, steady forward progression down the aisle, suggesting the user is moving or panning the camera forward.
New Information: The foreground is dominated by stacked items on the left shelf, including a large green and yellow bag marked "074". The right side shows more stacked brown boxes. The aisle floor is visible, leading toward a brighter area at the far end.
Continuity Points: This establishes the initial environment—a passage between shelves of goods. The user is clearly navigating through stored items.

Key continuation points:
- The contents and arrangement of the shelves on both the left and right sides.
- The direction of movement down the aisle.
- The user's focus, which seems to be surveying the items in the shelves.

**User looking for packages:** Yes, the entire context of the scene—being in an aisle surrounded by stacked boxes and bags—strongly suggests the user is looking for or organizing packages.

Frame 1
Setting/Scene: The setting remains indoors, in a narrow aisle lined with stacked cardboard boxes and bags, similar to Frame 0.
Action/Movement: The user's hand, holding a smartphone, is prominently featured in the foreground, suggesting the user is actively interacting with the phone while moving or pausing in the aisle. The phone screen is visible, displaying what appears to be a tracking or inventory interface.
New Information: The smartphone screen is the most notable addition. It displays a digital interface with fields like "Track remaining," "Items," "Packages," and numerical values, indicating the user is using an app related to logistics or inventory management. The hand holding the phone is positioned centrally.
Continuity Points: The environment (aisle, boxes) continues from Frame 0. The introduction of the phone and its specific interface strongly reinforces the narrative that the user is actively managing or searching for packages within this storage area.

Key continuation points:
- The user's continued interaction with the smartphone app.
- The contents of the surrounding boxes, checking for specific items or labels.
- The direction of movement relative to the phone's use.

video_description.response:
IDEO SUMMARY
Duration: 360 seconds (Total duration based on last frame's timestamp)

The video opens indoors, looking down a narrow aisle or hallway lined with shelves stacked with cardboard boxes. The camera moves slowly and steadily forward down this aisle, establishing a storage or back room setting. The foreground is dominated by stacked items on the left shelf, including a large green and yellow bag marked "074," while the right side features more stacked brown boxes. The initial context strongly suggests the user is engaged in an inventory or retrieval task, as the environment is entirely composed of stored goods.

The narrative progresses by showing the user actively managing packages within this storage area. In Frame 1, the user's hand holding a smartphone becomes prominent, displaying a digital interface with fields like "Track remaining," "Items," and "Packages," confirming the user is using a logistics or inventory management app while moving. This pattern continues through Frame 2, Frame 3, Frame 4, and Frame 5, where the camera maintains a steady forward progression down the aisle, with the user consistently consulting the phone while navigating the stacked boxes. The user is clearly engaged in a methodical inventory or retrieval task throughout this entire indoor sequence.

The scene undergoes a dramatic environmental shift in Frame 8, transitioning abruptly from the confined, indoor storage aisle to a bright, outdoor, natural setting dominated by lush green foliage and shrubs. The user's activity shifts away from inventory management. From Frame 9 through Frame 42, the camera continues to follow the user outdoors. The movement becomes slow, gentle, and exploratory, panning across the landscaped area. Throughout this entire outdoor segment, the smartphone remains visible, but its function changes; it is no longer displaying an inventory interface, suggesting the user is documenting or casually observing the surroundings. This pattern of outdoor exploration continues through Frame 43 to Frame 49.

The video concludes with the user still outdoors, continuing the slow, panning movement through the foliage in Frame 49. The overall narrative arc shows a clear transition from a highly structured, work-related task (package retrieval in a warehouse) to an unstructured, exploratory activity (walking through a garden).

Note: This summary is based on direct observation of the first frame combined with detailed notes from subsequent frames. The user is looking for packages only in the initial indoor sequence (Frames 0-7); after Frame 7, the user is not looking for packages.
